reviews.review_title: Yes! It’s awesome… but a few things
reviews.review_description:
This is exactly what I wanted it’s great , lots of guns to choose from , unlimited ammo, cool scenes. There was some glitching but overall really enjoyed it. 

Here’s what I would like to add:

-More glass bottle targets that reset automatically

-maybe some pop up fake animal targets 

-add hunting rifles 

-add the option to have NPC’s in the environments or in the store

-new environments 

-more reactive targets, buckets of flour, water bottles, tannerite, pumpkins, tv’s, fridges etc.

Im Very satisfied so far , can’t wait to see what gets added
10/10 game, its a very cool game but u guys need to add more atachments like supressors and 2 more things but the game is very nice

developer_response.body: Thank you for taking the time to play our game. I will definitely pass on your feedback to the dev team.

reviews.review_title: The app I’ve been looking for
reviews.review_description:
I loaded this app up and was fairly blown away by what it is. Simply put, it’s a gun range simulation. Nothing more, nothing less. You pick your range, gun, ammo, and target and shoot till you’re tired. With ammo and range prices these days, this app is amazing. Perfect? Not really. Satisfying? Absolutely. Probably the most practical app on the Quest, and will be the reason why I am using my Quest more. This scratches that itch so much better than other gun range apps I have tried. Well done 👍 

BTW I have been a Quest user for years and just now came across it. It should not have been so difficult to find. What’s up with that?

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for the review. unfortunately facebook will not let us publish to the quest main store  so we had to use sidequest to share our game. Perhaps that will change in future.

reviews.review_title: If you shoot in real life you need this
reviews.review_description:
As a 50 year old female who shoots in real life, I love the selection of ranges and guns.  Especially the metal plates range.  They are my favorites at the real range too.

I am new to gaming, I hijacked my husbands account  a few weeks ago,  and started adding games, that said, the controls on this game were super easy for me to figure out and the tutorial was very helpful.  

I also own Gun Club VR and after playing both I can say that this game is going to give them a run for the money.  It definitely fills a void in the market for people who are looking for a true range experience.  
I paid 14.99 for this game and had a heck of a time finding it as it was not listed in the game store but came up on the list at the bottom of an airplane game.  I don’t know how that works but a lot more people would own it if they knew it existed.  It does not even come up when I search by Gun Range.

As a new gamer I have one issue which is probably due to inexperience.  How do I get rid of the blue hands on the gun.  My finger is blocking the white dot on the sights.  I can still aim and shoot but it is in the way, my real finger is not there and I don’t think I have any kind of hand tracking on.  

Please add Sporting Clays and Trap in the future.  That would make the game perfect in my eyes.

This game is going to enable me to enjoy a sport that I love, target shooting, which I have had to cut back on due to ammo shortages and prices.

I can’t wait to see the official finished product.

Update - I figured out my hand issue.  It was user error as I suspected.  You need to hold your thumb near the xy or ab buttons on the controller if it is anywhere else it makes your thumb show as up which gets in the way.
This has become one of my 2 favorite apps.
￼ I want World War II weapons and The infinity ammo could be in the armory section￼￼￼￼

developer_response.body: thats great input Glaidd. ill pass along to our dev team!

reviews.review_title: Critical but honest review! DEVS Read!
reviews.review_description:
Hello, I received Range Day VR for free from the kind dev’s behind the project, the review might be slightly biased but I’ll do my best to be as neutral as possible, after reading tons of critical 3-2 star reviews I feel most of the criticism’s they bring to the table are valid and still valid to this day with all new updates included, I love guns and especially being able to toy around with them in VR but this game fails to do some of the basic expected things still but I can tell that feedback is going to be well appreciated so I will provide. The controls of each of the weapons might need to be tweaked slightly (add a function to the x or a button allowing for the use of the mag drop on certain weapons, use the same button to also release the charging handle as well on some weapons, add hk slaps for each of their respective weapons, add a mag pouch with mags in it for the currently selected weapon, tune and optimize the physics to reduce lag and frame drop issues, and tweak the ui’s for the menu’s and honestly this could be a solid game!) I have no issue with how the game is at the moment but I can definitely tell this can be a whole lot better than what it currently is, I hope the devs listen to this feedback as I feel it is all well written and thought out. Thanks again though for the free copy and please listen to my feedback if possible, If you like games like Gun Club VR and you can handle a bit of early access jank, this game is a great time waster polished or not! The base game is there and all that is needed now is improvements.


3 star review but I’d give a 3.5 if I could. 

- Hector

developer_response.body: Thank you for taking time to review and play our game.  Quick mag release can be done by pressing the thumb stick downward, Slide release and bolt release can be done by moving the thumb stick upwards on the touch controllers.  A new holster and mag pouch system is incoming in a future patch.  Our rift version of the game has this but we are having a rendering issue with quest that is still is being worked out.

reviews.review_description: I bought this app because it claimed to be a FPS and could be played while seated.  Alas, you can’t.  There's no settings menu where you could tell the app that you are playing while seated.  The tutorial claims that it shows the hand controllers to help the user learn the game.  Ehh, BUZZ, that also isn’t true as the controllers they use aren’t the Touch controllers used by Quest.  You’d think that loading a magazine into the handgun in the tutorial would be straight forward but it isn’t.  Expelling a spent clip and inserting a new one works but then you’re supposed to pull the back of the gun to install a bullet into the chamber but I was unable to grab hold of it and my now fully loaded gun couldn’t shoot.  I’m requesting a refund.  The app flat out doesn’t work.

developer_response.body:
Thesimonator

Sorry you are off to such a bad start.  Hand guns can be loaded by simply sliding the magazine into the magwell of the gun and pulling and releasing the slide.  Revolvers can be unloaded by putting your hand near the drum and clicking the A button to open, tilt the revolver backwards to allow all empty rounds to fall out and pickup and load bullets one at a time into the drum. Magazines can be removed from the gun by simply grabbing them from the magwell of the gun or by pulling the thumb stick downwards to eject them.  Bolt and slide release can be done manually by hand or by pushing the thumb stick forward.

reviews.review_description: This is a great gun simulator.  Not an arcade game. if your expecting that you will be greatly disappointed. I had trouble at first and finally decided to check out the tutorial which I should of done to begin with which solved some issues. I would like to see more variety of heavy machine guns and bolt action rifles.  If you want to feel like you’re at a gun range playing with real guns, then this may be your thing. It’s not perfect but we need more gun sims for quest! Look forward to more updates.

reviews.review_title: The most realistic shooting range game
reviews.review_description: This by far is the greatest shooting range game on quest. My only real complaint is the fact that my hands in game are farther out than my hands irl. I enjoy the guns in it and honestly dont know how this isnt a official game on the quest store yet

developer_response.body: Thank you for taking the time to play and review our game.  I will pass along your feedback to our dev team.  I know they are working on a large game patch for the next couple of months to add new ranges and guns to the game.  Perhaps they can add hand location adjustments to the options menu for this patch or a future patch.

reviews.review_description: Ya.. It's not without its glitches, but it is worth.. All unlocked. Just 16 ranges and alot of firearms and scopes.. Just learn and practice.. Propping your forearm agenst the front of controller is better than double grip. (Emerssion thing I guess) I like the yellow dot scope.. Sound is grest... I hope there are more updates addons??? Thank you to everyone involved with Range Day VR and bringing it to the Quest..

developer_response.body: Quite welcome. we do have a large update we are working on but not quite ready to announce yet.

reviews.review_description:
the controls are find the menu needs work and PLEASE ADD SNAP TURNING 
one time i tried to spawn i gun it spawned but wont spawn ammon
This game has potentail for multiplayer and shooting 
also add a hunting duck level it would be cool
overall i loved the game 
also missed opportunity at the intro and the crosshair at the logo in vr looks a little bit squiched

developer_response.body: Thank you for taking the time to play and review our game. Which firearm were you having trouble spawning ammo for? Want to get this feedback to our dev team. you can contact me at justin@invidiousstudios.com

reviews.review_description: physics suck, if a gun even touch something it will lag your game and bug out. Can't change your hight or anything. This game is a joke, I am going to refund it.

developer_response.body:
RealGothi

What you are describing happens when your guardian system is not calibrated correctly for the floor level.  Please rerun your guardian calibration verifying proper floor level and let us know if you are having this issue still. We can be reached at justin@invidiousstudios.com

reviews.review_description:
Definitely has potential as environments and graphics are well made.
It is a little buggy when trying to manually load guns.
Joystick is definitely the best way.
Gun also glitches out when trying to set down on bipod area.

Mainly got this for my son, I would definitely give a higher rating if there was an easier mode.
Idea for this would be a mode where guns are ready to go when spawning them, no messing around with safety or reloading, and have infinite ammo.

developer_response.body:
P-NuTTy

First, thank you for taking the time to play and review our game. Its greatly appreciated.  We have actually had similar feedback from other players with similar ideas.  While Range Day VR was designed to be more of a gun simulator then an arcade shooter we have decided to implement an actual arcade mode in the settings menu that will allow for auto gun reload, safety disable, and instant spawn with all attachments including magazines preloaded. It will be coming in a future update.  The next update adds new guns as well as new ranges releasing soon. Thank you for your feedback. Please feel free to email us anytime with any additional thoughts we can forward to the dev team at justin@invidiousstudios.com

reviews.review_description: Maybe it has 40 weapons but it’s better than I thought it would be

developer_response.body: Funny you should mention that.  More are on the way in the next few updates.  There are still some features, and guns that have not made it over from our rift version of the game to quest.   that’s in process now. We are a small indie game studio and really do appreciate you taking the time to play and review our game.

reviews.review_title: Lot of potential, not that great atm
reviews.review_description:
To start this, i will say that I got this game for free. I cannot give much input on the price as I did not pay for it. 
However, i can tell you that it's a decent game. The guns work most of the time (mostly the magazine loading that's complicated for me since the controllers keep hitting each other when trying to load a pistol) and can give you a "neat" gun shooty experience. It's alright. 
The bipod doesn't work at all for me tho and I have troubles with revolvers not wanting to fire after being reloaded. However it's a good enough game if you just want to shoot guns for a bit. 
Could be seriously improved, but it'll help pass the time.

developer_response.body: Jay. our latest game update should address the controller hitting each other issue during loading guns. New hand offsets have been created to stop this from happening as well as several other comfort of life fixes.  Give it a try and let us know your thoughts! Thank you for playing.

reviews.review_title: Good game what I would like to see us
reviews.review_description: More work done on bipods and bullet drop, also a more variety of calibers *cough* 50 browning machine gun *cough* but other than that hood game

reviews.review_title: Don't expect a game like Gunclub
reviews.review_description: Unfortunately I cannot give a good rating. Back then, I bought the game on itch immediately after it was published in the hope that it would be or become as good as gunclub. However, even now, 1 year later, it is still the same as it was then. Loveless menus, complicated controls (starting the range, choosing a weapon) and gameplay that quickly becomes boring. The developer doesn't seem to have the ambition to invest more time here and improve the game. Unfortunately not recommended for € 18.

developer_response.body: We have had 3 game updates since you published your review. We would love for you to replay Range Day VR and give us back your current thoughts.

reviews.review_description: It's pretty good but needs alot more polish, a bit of a high price for what you get.

If you are looking for a REAL range sim, this game is for you. Unlike gunclub you have to load rounds into the mags, the recoil is also more intense than gunclub. But gunclub is more polished overall. The graphics of range day are quite good, but the game also might run slow. Range day is also less expensive than gunclub. Gunclub,  being a full game, includes more weapons than range day, but range day is still in early access, so more guns will probably come. 4/5

developer_response.body: Thank you for taking time play and review our game. Further game optimizations are in the works as we are trying to carefully preserve the high end graphics our rift version of the game has as we bring all features to the quest port. more guns, updated physics code, and a port of our tactical vest and holsters from our rift port is coming in future updates.

reviews.review_description:
I've really enjoyed the game and my dad is always amazed by it, but I feel it's just slightly lacking in it's current state. So far the only accessories you can give to your gun are sights, and the fore grips are a little messy. Regardless it's still fairly realistic and fun to play.

Edit: ok so they responded and I didn't even expect that, now one of my favorite companies in the world for actually listening and talking to players. The full game is gonna be amazing!

developer_response.body: Actually a new updated accessory system is in development for a future update. Stay tuned!
